title: Sickle Cell Disease: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Sickle Cell Disease
Identify key diagnostic features of Sickle Cell Disease
Apply clinical reasoning to Sickle Cell Disease management
Recognize common pitfalls in Sickle Cell Disease

sections.title: Overview of Sickle Cell Disease
sections.content:
**Sickle Cell Disease**



**Key Points:**
• HbS polymerization under low O2 → sickle-shaped RBCs → vaso-occlusion + hemolysis
• Vaso-occlusive crisis: severe pain, triggered by dehydration, infection, cold, hypoxia
• Acute chest syndrome: chest pain + new infiltrate + fever — leading cause of death in adults with SCD

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• HbS polymerization under low O2 → sickle-shaped RBCs → vaso-occlusion + hemolysis
• Vaso-occlusive crisis: severe pain, triggered by dehydration, infection, cold, hypoxia

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• Acute chest syndrome: chest pain + new infiltrate + fever — leading cause of death in adults with SCD
• Functional asplenia by age 5 → encapsulated organism risk → penicillin prophylaxis until age 5
• Hydroxyurea: increases HbF, reduces crises; Gene therapy (Casgevy, Lyfgenia) approved 2023. Note: voxelotor was withdrawn Sept 2024 (improved Hb but no clinical benefit — surrogate endpoint teaching point)

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for Sickle Cell Disease:**

• Acute chest syndrome: chest pain + new infiltrate + fever — leading cause of death in adults with SCD
• Functional asplenia by age 5 → encapsulated organism risk → penicillin prophylaxis until age 5
• Hydroxyurea: increases HbF, reduces crises; Gene therapy (Casgevy, Lyfgenia) approved 2023. Note: voxelotor was withdrawn Sept 2024 (improved Hb but no clinical benefit — surrogate endpoint teaching point)

options.explanation: Hydroxyurea: increases HbF, reduces crises; Gene therapy (Casgevy, Lyfgenia) approved 2023. Note: voxelotor was withdrawn Sept 2024 (improved Hb but no clinical benefit — surrogate endpoint teaching point)

options.text: Immediate surgical intervention in all cases

mcqs.question: A patient presents with findings consistent with Sickle Cell Disease. Which finding would you expect?

options.text: Vaso-occlusive crisis: severe pain, triggered by dehydration, infection, cold, hypoxia
options.explanation: Vaso-occlusive crisis: severe pain, triggered by dehydration, infection, cold, hypoxia

mcqs.stem: A 35-year-old presents with petechiae, confusion, renal insufficiency, fever, and platelets of 18,000 with schistocytes on smear. What is the most critical immediate intervention?

options.text: Emergent plasma exchange (plasmapheresis) — do not wait for ADAMTS13 results
options.explanation: This pentad (thrombocytopenia, microangiopathic hemolytic anemia with schistocytes, neurologic symptoms, renal dysfunction, fever) is classic TTP. Per ASH guidelines, plasma exchange must be initiated IMMEDIATELY — mortality without treatment exceeds 90%. Do NOT wait for ADAMTS13 activity levels; start treatment on clinical suspicion.

options.text: Platelet transfusion to prevent intracranial hemorrhage
options.explanation: Platelet transfusion in TTP is CONTRAINDICATED (relative) as it can fuel thrombotic microangiopathy and worsen organ damage. Platelets are consumed in microvascular thrombi, not bleeding.

TTP is distinguished from DIC by NORMAL coagulation studies (PT, PTT, fibrinogen). DIC shows prolonged PT/PTT and low fibrinogen. Treatment differs fundamentally — TTP requires plasmapheresis, not anticoagulation.

options.text: Obtain ADAMTS13 activity level and treat based on results
options.explanation: ADAMTS13 testing takes 2-3 days. With suspected TTP, mortality approaches 90% without treatment. Plasma exchange must begin on clinical suspicion while awaiting confirmatory testing.
